dispatcher_boundary.decision: operations owns runtime dispatch; cpk-server receives a configured dispatcher; interpreters own concrete effect implementations
must_not_happen: cpk-server must not own Docker behavior
core must not import Docker SDK or concrete runtime effect code
operations must not import concrete interpreter implementations except in explicit composition tests
product descriptors must not contain host Docker socket paths or runtime credentials

ordering_laws: #900 must precede #902 so operations owns dispatcher before Docker SDK implementation exists
#901 must precede #902 so the current operations-local adapter behavior is stabilized before replacing CLI mechanics
#904 and #905 are parallel after probe foundation because configuration and secret materialization are distinct runtime surfaces
#908 must follow #907 so cpk-server receives a proven dispatcher/interpreter seam instead of inventing one
#910 is a readiness dry run only; full recursive cpk-server acceptance remains #676

can_move_to_interpreters: Docker ownership-label checks if kept independent of durable stores
cpk_server_composition_only: Bootstrap parsing for explicit runtime authority
Construction or receipt of a configured RuntimeInterpreterDispatcher
Unsupported default when no runtime authority is configured
HTTP/MCP/auth process wrapper over the same operations services
frozen_inspiration_only: RuntimeState as the primary workflow state
RuntimePlan as a bypass around durable ActivityPlan execution

law_cards.observable_law: Runtime kind dispatch is derived from the pinned base or desired graph in ActivityRealizationContext, not from mutable current graph truth.
negative_cases: missing interpreter returns unsupported evidence
non-Docker runtime is not sent to DockerRuntimeInterpreter

law_cards.observable_law: StartRuntime and ReconcileRuntime inspect or create exactly the owned Docker network with workspace, runtime, graph, plan, and owner labels.
negative_cases: foreign network label conflict fails before mutation

law_cards.observable_law: StartNode pulls digest-pinned OCI material, reuses owned running containers, restarts owned stopped containers, and separates stop from removal.
negative_cases: unowned container collision fails before pull or run
compute removal is explicit and separate from stop

law_cards.observable_law: Runtime execution accepts immutable image references and rejects non-digest OCI image material for acceptance.
negative_cases: tag-only image is unsupported for realization acceptance
node metadata image mismatch conflicts before mutation

law_cards.observable_law: Requirement socket environment values are derived from graph edges and product runtime contracts, not product-specific Docker branches.
negative_cases: unconnected required sockets fail before runtime
optional observer socket remains absent when not connected

law_cards.observable_law: Retained data mounts create or reuse only owned-compatible volumes and teardown preserves retained data unless explicit data destruction is requested.
negative_cases: foreign volume collision fails before pull or run
retained data is not removed as compute cleanup

law_cards.observable_law: Secret environment delivery resolves only at the runtime boundary and fails before mutation when a resolver is missing or denies access.
negative_cases: secret bytes never enter descriptors, evidence, logs, labels, or command arguments

law_cards.observable_law: Secret-file materialization writes secret bytes only through bounded runtime transport and preserves owned volumes for uncertain recovery.
negative_cases: missing or denied secret fails before volume mutation
container start failure cleans owned secret volume only when result is known

law_cards.observable_law: Configuration artifacts are materialized before container start as immutable read-only owned resources with digest verification and deterministic replay.
negative_cases: wrong digest fails without starting
partial materialization is uncertain and cleanup is artifact-only

law_cards.observable_law: HTTP health checks are bounded, redirect-free, retry-aware, and status-driven against the provider endpoint selected by the verification contract.
negative_cases: failed health does not rewrite process-start observation
missing endpoint fails before probe attempt

law_cards.observable_law: Postgres readiness is semantic query readiness and must not be represented as HTTP health or mere TCP reachability.
negative_cases: unreachable Postgres does not run HTTP
deadline exhaustion records transport evidence

law_cards.observable_law: Host publication is explicit typed graph material; private-only is the default and UDP/TCP publication semantics remain distinct.
negative_cases: fixed port collisions fail at authoring
Docker private endpoint is distinct from host-observed endpoint

law_cards.observable_law: Runtime endpoint observations are recorded as operational evidence and do not rewrite desired graph truth.
negative_cases: process start is not health
host-local and public endpoint facts are separate

law_cards.observable_law: Existing Docker resources may be reused only when stable owner/workspace/runtime/node/product/descriptor/data labels are compatible.
negative_cases: changed material conflicts instead of retargeting same name
unowned name collision is never mutated

law_cards.observable_law: Timeouts and unknown result windows become explicit uncertain outcomes without publishing command output or blindly replaying destructive effects.
negative_cases: failure after known removal is uncertain
partial materialization preserves owned evidence for recovery

law_cards.observable_law: Cleanup removes only proven-owned control-plane-kit runtime resources and refuses broad Docker prune behavior.
negative_cases: Pottery Factory and unrelated resources are preserved
retained volumes survive compute teardown

law_cards.observable_law: Hosted cpk-server defaults to unsupported runtime execution and may receive a dispatcher only through explicit runtime authority configuration.
negative_cases: cpk-server image does not mutate Docker merely because it is cpk-server
host Docker socket paths do not enter product descriptors

law_cards.observable_law: A DockerRuntimeAgent may become the preferred authority holder for recursive/self-hosted Docker mutation instead of direct socket mount inside every cpk-server.
negative_cases: runtime authority is not implicit
agent route security must be authenticated and bounded

law_cards.observable_law: Parent cpk-server admits child cpk-server as an ordinary product descriptor and does not own child graph truth or activity history.
negative_cases: no privileged self-registration
no recursive parent proxy requirement

law_cards.observable_law: AWS, GCP, Kubernetes, and other runtimes interpret the same core topology and activity language through explicit runtime authority and substrate-specific adapters.
negative_cases: Docker behavior must not become the graph model
cloud credentials do not enter descriptors

docker_sdk_coverage.network_lifecycle: covered by Docker SDK network inspect/create/remove
docker_sdk_coverage.container_lifecycle: covered by Docker SDK image pull, container create/start/stop/remove, network aliases, labels, mounts
docker_sdk_coverage.volume_lifecycle: covered by Docker SDK volume inspect/create/remove and labels
docker_sdk_coverage.host_publication: covered by Docker SDK port bindings; explicit typed transport mapping must be preserved
docker_sdk_coverage.inspection: covered by Docker SDK object attrs; normalize to current DockerResourceInspection
docker_sdk_coverage.logs: available but must remain bounded if surfaced
docker_sdk_coverage.timeouts: Docker SDK supports request timeouts at client/API layers but interpreter must normalize uncertain windows explicitly
docker_sdk_coverage.secret_stdin_materialization: not directly solved by high-level API; likely requires exec/helper-container or archive APIs with strict no-argv proof
docker_sdk_coverage.configuration_materialization: not directly solved by high-level API; likely requires volume helper, archive APIs, or a constrained helper container

implementation_hazards: Do not move ActivityRealizationContext into interpreters; it is operations-owned durable material.
Do not make DockerRuntimeInterpreter expose old up/down as the production workflow path.
Do not let cpk-server import Docker SDK directly as its execution model.
Do not use product-specific branches for hello, router, multiplexer, cpk-server, or postgres-server.
Do not weaken secret/config laws when replacing CLI helpers with Docker SDK mechanics.
Do not treat Docker socket mount as ordinary descriptor data.

next_handoff.summary: Scaffold control-plane-kit-interpreters with Docker-first unittest harness and architecture boundaries. Keep the package root lightweight. Do not implement Docker SDK behavior until #900 and #901 land.
